mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing units starts above 600,000 signals a strengthening demand for residential real estate, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST > 600,000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below 100 signals a weakening supply chain, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO < 100 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in initial unemployment claims above 250,000 signals a weakening labor market, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS < 250,000 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate below 1% and a 3-month increase in the CPI above 2% signal a monetary easing cycle with inflationary pressures, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with inflationary pressures
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods with both FEDFUNDS < 1% and CPIAUCSL > 2% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 3% and a 3-month increase in real disposable personal income above 2% signal a strengthening demand for consumer goods, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with both GDPC1 > 3% and RSAFS > 2% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 5% signals a tightening labor market, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE < 5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the CPI above 3% and a 3-month decline in industrial production below 100 signal a supply chain disruption with inflationary pressures, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods with both CPIAUCSL > 3% and INDPRO < 100 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
